revision.text: #redirect[[en:Tamaru-hime]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''多満留姫'''（たまるひめ）は、[[諏訪|州羽地方]]の民間伝承の[[神]]。
== 概要 == [[洩矢神]]の娘で、[[建御名方神]]の第二子・[[出早雄命]]に嫁いだとされる<ref>[[長野県神社庁]]</ref>。
『神長官守矢氏系譜』では出早雄命の后となっているが、建御名方神に嫁いで出早雄命を生んだとする説もある<ref>諏訪神社旧記</ref>。
'''多留姫命'''との記述もあるが<ref>諏訪史料叢書.巻28</ref>、通常'''[[多留比売命|多留比売神]]'''は建御名方神の御子神とされる<ref>諏訪神社略縁起</ref>。
== 系譜 == [[洩矢神]]の娘で兄に[[洩宅神]]がいる<ref>諏訪史料叢書.巻28</ref>。
[[建御名方神]]と[[八坂刀売神]]の子・[[出早雄命]]に嫁ぎ、[[可毛羽神]]、[[若木比売神]]、[[草奈井比売神]]、[[出早比売命]]、[[八縣宿禰神]]などの子がいる。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[洩矢神]] *[[洩宅神]] *[[建御名方神]] *[[八坂刀売神]]
[[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]]

== 系譜 == [[洩矢神]]の娘で兄に[[洩宅神]]がいる<ref>諏訪史料叢書.巻28</ref>。
[[建御名方神]]と[[八坂刀売神]]の子・[[出早雄命]]に嫁ぎ、[[可毛羽神]]、[[若木比売神]]、[[草奈井比売神]]、[[出早比売命]]、[[八縣宿禰神]]などの子がいる。

（洩矢神から八櫛神まで）}}]] [[洩矢神]]の娘で兄に[[洩宅神]]がいる諏訪史料叢書.巻28{{要ページ番号|date=2018年8月}}。 [[建御名方神]]と[[八坂刀売神]]の子・[[出早雄命|伊豆速男命]]に嫁ぎ、[[可毛羽神]]、[[若木比売神]]、[[草奈井比売神]]、[[出早比売命|伊都速比売命]]、[[八縣宿禰神|佐和恵多良六老彦神]]などの子がいる。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[洩矢神]] *[[洩宅神]] *[[建御名方神]] *[[八坂刀売神]] {{デフォルトソート:たまるひめ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:諏訪信仰|神たまるひめ]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox deity | name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | aliases = Tamaru-hime no Mikoto (多留姫命) | alternate_names = | divine_nature = | father = {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} | consort = {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}} | children = {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}}, {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}, {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}}, etc. | shrine = {{ill|Taru-hime Shrine|ja|多留姫神社}}? | related_clans = {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} }}
'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} of {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
[[File:Taruhime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]
== Overview == Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, who is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}.<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>
In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya Clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>
The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>
== Lineage == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} lineage (from {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神}}).]]
Tamaru-hime is the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>
She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they had the following children: * {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神}} * {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神}} * {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}} * {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}} * {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} * {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}

{{Infobox deity | name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | aliases = Tamaru-hime no Mikoto (多留姫命) | alternate_names = | divine_nature = | father = {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} | consort = {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}} | children = {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}}, {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}, {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}}, etc. | shrine = {{ill|Taru-hime Shrine|ja|多留姫神社}}? | related_clans = {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} }}
'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} of {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
[[File:Taruhime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]
== Overview == Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, who is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}.<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>
In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya Clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>
The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>
== Lineage == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} lineage (from {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神}}).]]
Tamaru-hime is the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>
She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they had the following children: * {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神}} * {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神}} * {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}} * {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}} * {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}

{{Infobox deity | name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | aliases = Tamaru-hime no Mikoto (多留姫命) | alternate_names = | divine_nature = | father = {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} | consort = {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}} | children = {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}}, {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}, {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}}, etc. | shrine = {{ill|Taru-hime Shrine|ja|多留姫神社}}? | related_clans = {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} }}
'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} of {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
[[File:Taruhime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]
== Overview == Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, who is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}.<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>
In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya Clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>
The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>
== Lineage == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} lineage (from {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神}}).]]
Tamaru-hime is the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>
She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they had the following children: * {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神}} * {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神}} * {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}} * {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}} * {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}

She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they had the following children: * {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神}} * {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神}} * {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}} * {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}} * {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox deity | name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | aliases = Tamaru-hime no Mikoto (多留姫命) | alternate_names = | divine_nature = | father = {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} | consort = {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}} | children = {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}}, {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}, {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}}, etc. | shrine = {{ill|Taru-hime Shrine|ja|多留姫神社}}? | related_clans = {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} }}
'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} of {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
[[File:Taruhime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]
== Overview == Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, who is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}.<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>
In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya Clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>
The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>
== Lineage == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} lineage (from {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神}}).]]
Tamaru-hime is the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>
She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they had the following children: * {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神}} * {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神}} * {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}} * {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}} * {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} * {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}

{{Infobox deity | name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | aliases = Tamaru-hime no Mikoto (多留姫命) | alternate_names = | divine_nature = | father = {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} | consort = {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}} | children = {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}}, {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}, {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}}, etc. | shrine = {{ill|Taru-hime Shrine|ja|多留姫神社}}? | related_clans = {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} }}
'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} of {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
[[File:Taruhime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]
== Overview == Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, who is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}.<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>
In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya Clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>
The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>
== Lineage == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} lineage (from {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神}}).]]
Tamaru-hime is the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>
She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they had the following children: * {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神}} * {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神}} * {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}} * {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}} * {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} * {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}

{{Infobox deity | name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | aliases = Tamaru-hime no Mikoto (多留姫命) | alternate_names = | divine_nature = | father = {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} | consort = {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}} | children = {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}}, {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}, {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}}, etc. | shrine = {{ill|Taru-hime Shrine|ja|多留姫神社}}? | related_clans = {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} }}
'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} of {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
[[File:Taruhime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]
== Overview == Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, who is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}.<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>
In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya Clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>
The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>
== Lineage == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} lineage (from {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神}}).]]
Tamaru-hime is the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>
She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they had the following children: * {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神}} * {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神}} * {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}} * {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}} * {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} * {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}

Together, they had the following children: * {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神}} * {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神}} * {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}} * {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}} * {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}

{{Infobox deity | name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | aliases = Tamaru-hime no Mikoto (多留姫命) | alternate_names = | divine_nature = | father = {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} | consort = {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}} | children = {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}}, {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}, {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}}, etc. | shrine = {{ill|Taru-hime Shrine|ja|多留姫神社}}? | related_clans = {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} }}
'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} of {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
[[File:Taruhime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]
== Overview == Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, who is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}.<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>
In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya Clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>
The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>
== Lineage == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} lineage (from {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神}}).]]
Tamaru-hime is the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>
She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they had the following children: * {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神}} * {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神}} * {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}} * {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}} * {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}

She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they had the following children: * {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神}} * {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神}} * {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}} * {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}} * {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}

{{Infobox deity | name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | aliases = Tamaru-hime no Mikoto (多留姫命) | alternate_names = | divine_nature = | father = {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} | consort = {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}} | children = {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}}, {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}, {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}}, etc. | shrine = {{ill|Taru-hime Shrine|ja|多留姫神社}}? | related_clans = {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} }}
'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} of {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
[[File:Taruhime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]
== Overview == Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, who is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}.<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>
In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya Clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>
The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>
== Lineage == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} lineage (from {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神}}).]]
Tamaru-hime is the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>
She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they had the following children: * {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神}} * {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神}} * {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}} * {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}} * {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}

[[File:Taruhime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]
== Overview == Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, who is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}.<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>
In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya Clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>
The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>
== Lineage == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} lineage (from {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神}}).]]
Tamaru-hime is the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>
She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they had the following children: * {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神}} * {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神}} * {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}} * {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}} * {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} * {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}

{{Infobox deity | name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Tamaru-hime (多満留姫) | aliases = Tamaru-hime no Mikoto (多留姫命) | alternate_names = | divine_nature = | father = {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} | consort = {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}} | children = {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}}, {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}, {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}}, etc. | shrine = {{ill|Taru-hime Shrine|ja|多留姫神社}}? | related_clans = {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} }}
'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} of {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
[[File:Taruhime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]
== Overview == Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, who is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}.<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>
In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya Clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>
The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>
== Lineage == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} lineage (from {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神}}).]]
Tamaru-hime is the daughter of {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya Clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>
She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命}}, the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they had the following children: * {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神}} * {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神}} * {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神}} * {{ill|Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命}} * {{ill|Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} * {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 多満留姫 | 世代名 = | 先代= | 次代= | 神祇 = [[国津神]] | 全名 = 多満留姫 | 別名 = 多留姫命 | 別称 = | 神格 = | 父 = [[洩矢神]] | 配偶者 = [[出早雄命|伊豆速男命]] | 子 = [[出早比売命|伊都速比売命]]、[[八縣宿禰神]]、[[草奈井比売神]] 等 | 宮 = | 神社 = [[多留姫神社]]？ | 関連氏族 = [[諏訪氏]]、[[守矢氏]]}} '''多満留姫'''（たまるひめ）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪地方]]の民間伝承（[[諏訪信仰]]）の[[神 (神道)|女神]]。 [[File:Taruhime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|多留姫神社]] == 概要 == [[洩矢神]]の娘で、[[建御名方神]]の[[出早雄命|出速雄神]]に嫁いだとされる「神社紹介、諏訪支部[[洩矢神社]]」『[[長野県神社庁]]公式ホームページ』「神長守矢氏系譜」『諏訪史料叢書.巻28』諏訪教育会、昭和11年、31頁。。『神長官守矢氏系譜』では出早雄命の后となっているが、一方別の史料では建御名方神に嫁いで出早雄神を生んだともされる延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.続編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、19頁。。 '''多留姫命'''との記述もあるが「神長守矢氏系譜」『諏訪史料叢書.巻28』諏訪教育会、昭和11年、34頁。、通常'''[[多留比売命|多留比売神]]'''は建御名方神の御子神とされる守矢実久「健御名方命御系圖」『諏訪神社略縁起』中村甚之助、明治53年、10頁。。 == 系譜 == [[ファイル:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|サムネイル|{{Center|[[守矢氏]]の最初の五代
（洩矢神から八櫛神まで）}}]] [[洩矢神]]の娘で兄に[[洩宅神]]がいる「神長守矢氏系譜」『諏訪史料叢書.巻28』諏訪教育会、昭和11年、33、34頁。。 [[建御名方神]]と[[八坂刀売神]]の子・[[出早雄命|伊豆速男命]]に嫁ぎ、[[可毛羽神]]、[[若木比売神]]、[[草奈井比売神]]、[[出早比売命|伊都速比売命]]、[[八縣宿禰神|佐和恵多良六老彦神]]などの子がいる。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[洩矢神]] *[[洩宅神]] *[[建御名方神]] *[[八坂刀売神]] {{デフォルトソート:たまるひめ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:諏訪信仰|神たまるひめ]]

| children = {{ill|Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsuhaya-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanoi-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135493035}}, etc.
| shrine = {{ill|Taru-hime Shrine|ja|多留姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taru-hime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多留姫の滝|1=Taruhime Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430989}}?

}}

'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]].

[[File:Tamaru-hime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]

== Overview ==
Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]].<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moriya Shrine|lt=Moriya Shrine|lt=Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moreya Shrine|qq=}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Shrine Agency|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>

In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genealogy of the Moriya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>

The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taru-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>

== Lineage ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} lineage (from [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).]]

Tamaru-hime is the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>

She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]]. Together, they had the following children:
* {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameba no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135493216}}
* {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakagi-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135492776}}
* {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanoi-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135493035}}
* {{ill|Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsuhaya-hime|qq=}}
* {{ill|Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|qq=}}

== Overview ==
Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]].<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moriya Shrine|lt=Moriya Shrine|lt=Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moreya Shrine|qq=}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Shrine Agency|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>

In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genealogy of the Moriya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>

The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taru-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>

== Lineage ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} lineage (from [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).]]

Tamaru-hime is the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>

, the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]]. Together, they had the following children:

Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]].<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moriya Shrine|lt=Moriya Shrine|lt=Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moreya Shrine|qq=}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Shrine Agency|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>

In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genealogy of the Moriya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>

The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taru-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>

Tamaru-hime is the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>

'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]].

[[File:Tamaru-hime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]

== Overview ==
Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]].<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moriya Shrine|lt=Moriya Shrine|lt=Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moreya Shrine|qq=}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Shrine Agency|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>

In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genealogy of the Moriya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>

The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taru-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>

'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]].

[[File:Tamaru-hime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]

== Overview ==
Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]].<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moriya Shrine|lt=Moriya Shrine|lt=Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moreya Shrine|qq=}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Shrine Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>

In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>

The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>

== Lineage ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} lineage (from [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).]]

Tamaru-hime is the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>

She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]]. Together, they had the following children:
* {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameba no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}
* {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakagi-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}
* {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanoi-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}
* {{ill|Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsuhaya-hime|qq=}}
* {{ill|Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|qq=}}

'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]].

[[File:Tamaru-hime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]

== Overview ==
Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]].<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moriya Shrine|lt=Moriya Shrine|lt=Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moreya Shrine|qq=}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Shrine Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>

In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>

The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>

== Lineage ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} lineage (from [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).]]

Tamaru-hime is the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>

She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]]. Together, they had the following children:
* {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameba no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}
* {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakagi-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}
* {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanoi-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}
* {{ill|Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsuhaya-hime|qq=}}
* {{ill|Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|qq=}}

'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]].

[[File:Tamaru-hime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]

== Overview ==
Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]].<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moriya Shrine|lt=Moriya Shrine|lt=Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moreya Shrine|qq=}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Shrine Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>

In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>

The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>

== Lineage ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} lineage (from [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).]]

Tamaru-hime is the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>

She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]]. Together, they had the following children:
* {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameba no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}
* {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakagi-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}
* {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanoi-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}
* {{ill|Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsuhaya-hime|qq=}}
* {{ill|Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|qq=}}

'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]].

[[File:Tamaru-hime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]

== Overview ==
Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]].<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moriya Shrine|lt=Moriya Shrine|lt=Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moreya Shrine|qq=}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Shrine Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>

In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>

The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>

== Lineage ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} lineage (from [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).]]

Tamaru-hime is the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>

She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]]. Together, they had the following children:
* {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameba no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}
* {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakagi-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}
* {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanoi-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}
* {{ill|Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsuhaya-hime|qq=}}
* {{ill|Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|qq=}}

'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]].

[[File:Tamaru-hime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]

== Overview ==
Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]].<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moriya Shrine|lt=Moriya Shrine|lt=Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moreya Shrine|qq=}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Shrine Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>

In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>

The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>

== Lineage ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} lineage (from [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).]]

Tamaru-hime is the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>

She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]]. Together, they had the following children:
* {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameba no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}

'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]].

[[File:Tamaru-hime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]

== Overview ==
Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]].<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moriya Shrine|lt=Moriya Shrine|lt=Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moreya Shrine|qq=}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Shrine Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>

In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>

The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>

== Lineage ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} lineage (from [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).]]

Tamaru-hime is the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>

She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]]. Together, they had the following children:
* {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameba no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}
* {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakagi-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}
* {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanoi-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}
* {{ill|Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsuhaya-hime|qq=}}
* {{ill|Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|qq=}}

== Lineage ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} lineage (from [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).]]

'''Tamaru-hime''' (多満留姫) is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} (earthly deity) and a goddess in the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a belief system native to the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]].

[[File:Tamaru-hime_Shrine_1.jpg|thumb|200px|Taru-hime Shrine]]

== Overview ==
Tamaru-hime is said to be the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]].<ref>"Shincho Shrine Introduction: {{ill|Moriya Shrine|lt=Moriya Shrine|lt=Moreya Shrine|ja|洩矢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moreya Shrine|qq=}}," {{ill|Nagano Shrine Agency|ja|長野県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Shrine Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} official website.</ref><ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 31–34.</ref>

In the {{ill|Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja|神長守矢氏系譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genealogy of the Moriya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tamaru-hime is described as the consort of Izuhaya no Mikoto. Another source alternatively claims that she was married to [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and gave birth to Izuhaya no Mikoto.<ref>Yonekawa Kazuhiko, "Revised Suwa Genealogy," ''Suwa Genealogy, Vol. 2,'' Iida Kotaro, 1921, p. 19.</ref>

The goddess is sometimes referred to as '''Tamaru-hime no Mikoto''' (多留姫命). However, in most contexts, she is distinguished from {{ill|Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja|多留比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taru-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is commonly regarded as a child of Takeminakata.<ref>Morii Mikio, "Genealogy of Takeminakata," ''Suwa Shrine Historical Records,'' Nakamura Jinnosuke, 1910, p. 10.</ref>

== Lineage ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|center|The first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} lineage (from [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] to {{ill|Yasugami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).]]

Tamaru-hime is the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>

She married {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]]. Together, they had the following children:
* {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameba no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}
* {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakagi-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}
* {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanoi-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}
* {{ill|Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsuhaya-hime|qq=}}
* {{ill|Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|qq=}}

Tamaru-hime is the daughter of [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] and the sister of {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref>"Genealogy of the Moriya clan," {{ill|Suwa Historical Association|ja|諏訪教育会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Historical Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1936, pp. 33–34.</ref>

Together, they had the following children: * {{ill|Kameba no Kami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameba no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}} * {{ill|Wakagi-hime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakagi-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}} * {{ill|Kusanoi-hime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanoi-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}} * {{ill|Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhayahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Itsuhaya-hime|ja|出早比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsuhaya-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yagata no Sukune no Kami|lt=Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugata no Sukune no Kami|qq=}}
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] * {{ill|Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=}} * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] * [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]]
